perturbation.original_text: WCC grants a limited, non-exclusive license to XLI to use the West Coast Customs name, image, likeness and signature, including specific West Coast Customs Trademarks (including West Coast Customs logo trademarks and approved West Coast Customs vehicle imagery and trademarks) (collectively the "Trademarks") during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site event advertising, only in connection with the PROMOTION, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time XLI shall cease all use of the Trademarks.
perturbation.changed_text: WCC grants a fully unrestricted, exclusive license to XLI to use the West Coast Customs name, image, likeness and signature, including specific West Coast Customs Trademarks (including West Coast Customs logo trademarks and approved West Coast Customs vehicle imagery and trademarks) (collectively the "Trademarks") during the term of this Agreement in connection with any form of advertising or promotion, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall remain in effect indefinitely, even after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time XLI retains all rights to use the Trademarks.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the license granted to XLI. Originally, the license was 'limited, non-exclusive' and terminated upon the agreement's expiration. The modification makes it 'fully unrestricted, exclusive' and perpetual, conflicting with Section 1.1.2 that grants WCC a license that is also non-exclusive and is automatically terminated after the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XLI grants WCC a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the registered trademark "Light Sheets" during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site advertising, for WCC and its Event(s) during the term of this Agreement, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time WCC agrees to cease all use of the registered trademark "Light Sheets". In further consideration of this Agreement, WCC agrees that it shall not in any way disparage the brand name "XLI", nor any of XLI's parent, subsidiary, or affiliated companies or its or their products. XLI shall provide WCC with all the applicable logos for the "Light Sheets" trademark. XLI shall have the right of prior written approval over all uses of the trademark "XLI" by WCC. The parties understand and acknowledge the importance of protecting the goodwill associated with their respective trademarks. Consequently, WCC hereby assigns to XLI all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with WCC's use of XLI's trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of XLI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant in-text contradiction. The original text granted XLI a 'limited, non-exclusive' license that terminates upon the agreement's expiration. The changed text grants a 'fully unrestricted, exclusive' license that remains in effect indefinitely. This directly contradicts the license granted to WCC in the Contradicted Text (Section 1.1.2), which is 'limited, non-exclusive' and terminates automatically upon the agreement's expiration. The term change from 'limited, non-exclusive' to 'fully unrestricted, exclusive' creates a conflict that would cause legal issues and uncertainty. Additionally, the initial text has the licence terminating after the agreement while the changed text has it remaining in effect, which is incompatible. Thus, a strong, direct, and impactful conflict exists within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Any termination allowed by this Agreement will take place only after written notice of default has been given to the defaulting party, providing such party with thirty (30) days in which to cure the default.
perturbation.changed_text: Any termination allowed by this Agreement will take place immediately without any prior notice or cure period.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause by eliminating the requirement for written notice and a cure period before termination. This contradicts with the previous sentence where it says: 'This Agreement may be terminated upon written notice by either party in the event of a default by the other party in the performance of any term or condition of this Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination upon Breach. This Agreement may be terminated upon written notice by either party in the event of a default by the other party in the performance of any term or condition of this Agreement. Any termination allowed by this Agreement will take place immediately without any prior notice or cure period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly eliminates the requirement for written notice and a cure period before termination, which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that the agreement may be terminated upon written notice, therefore creating an in-text contradiction according to the guidelines.

perturbation.original_text: WCC agrees to provide the following to XLI as part of this agreement: 1. WCC will incorporate XLI into other existing WCC relationships and opportunities as appropriate for the products and technology. 2. XLI and the products and technology will receive direct brand mention during any episode produced for 2016 by WCC to the extent the products and technology are used in the production. 3. XLI representatives will be included in WCC media days and will make guest appearances as the support for "Light Sheets". 4. XLI shall receive mentions in all applicable WCC press releases that relate to the "Light Sheets".
perturbation.changed_text: WCC agrees to provide the following to XLI as part of this agreement: 1. WCC may, at its sole discretion, consider incorporating XLI into other existing WCC relationships and opportunities. 2. XLI and the products and technology will receive indirect brand mention if WCC feels like it, and it will be hidden in the show, during any episode produced for 2016 by WCC to the extent the products and technology are used in the production. 3. WCC will prevent XLI representatives from appearing in WCC media days and will refuse any guest appearances as the support for "Light Sheets". 4. XLI shall not receive any mentions in all applicable WCC press releases that relate to the "Light Sheets".
perturbation.explanation: The modification of this section introduces contradictions by changing the mandatory obligations of WCC into discretionary actions or outright prohibitions. Specifically, changing 'WCC will incorporate XLI' to 'WCC may consider incorporating XLI' makes the action optional. Moreover, stating that 'XLI shall not receive any mentions' in press releases directly contradicts the original text. These changes create conflicting terms in Attachment A with the original intentions. This contradicts with 1.2 Additional Promotional Support.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Promotional Support. In addition to the items set forth in Section 1.1.3, WCC shall promote XLI as outlined in the Sponsor Benefits Schedule, attached hereto as Attachment A, which shall be deemed an extension of this Agreement. In the event of a conflict between said Sponsor Benefits Schedule and this Agreement, the Sponsor Benefits Schedule shall control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces direct contradictions with the "Additional Promotional Support" clause. The original text commits WCC to specific promotional activities for XLI, as detailed in Attachment A. The modification changes mandatory actions to discretionary ones or outright prohibitions (e.g., preventing XLI representatives from appearing at media days and stating that XLI shall not receive any mentions in press releases), which directly clashes with the Sponsor Benefits Schedule (Attachment A) that WCC is supposed to adhere to and promote XLI by. This contradiction undermines the promotional support that was initially agreed upon, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
